element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Core offensive tool introduced immediately for enemy engagement
Progression mechanic; red orbs upgrade to triple fireball attack
Defensive recovery item; restores health after damage
Basic melee threat requiring timing and avoidance
Larger, more dangerous enemies with distinct attack patterns
Flying threats requiring aerial combat awareness
Large environmental obstacles with offensive capabilities
Heavily armored threats requiring sustained damage
Complex multi-segment enemies with segmented attack zones
High-speed mobile threat combining movement and ranged attacks
Strategic panic item that destroys all on-screen enemies
Defensive utility allowing enemy projectile deflection
Boss reward system; 9 pieces total leading to final treasure
Mandatory climactic encounters at each level conclusion
Ultimate challenge defending final treasure map piece

typical_relief_triggers: Defeating boss and receiving map piece
Deploying Spike Bomb for screen clear
typical_tension_triggers: Entering new level with unfamiliar enemy types
Health depleted to critical single-hit state
Approaching final levels with dangerous enemy roster
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Graduated escalation across 9 levels with enemy variety increasing in danger and complexity; boss gates provide natural tension peaks and relief valleys; power-up frequency allows manageable difficulty progression

difficulty_curve.early_game: Gentle introduction with basic fireball mechanics against sword monks and simple dinosaurs; healing available; two-hit health pool allows learning curve
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Enemy variety expands to include djinn, fish men, and gargoyles with more aggressive patterns; power-up frequency decreases; map piece collection provides motivational structure
difficulty_curve.late_game: Dangerous enemy roster (centipedes, skeleton warriors, will-o-wisps, rock creatures) with complex behaviors; resource scarcity increases; final boss presents ultimate mechanical challenge
notable_spikes: Transition from Level 3 to Level 4 (Level 4 completely redesigned from arcade version)
Famicom version spike due to limited continues (only 2 vs NES unlimited)

notable_level_patterns.description: Each of 9 levels follows linear forward progression with climactic boss encounter required to obtain treasure map piece; boss defeat gates level completion

Early levels feature basic monks and dinosaurs; mid-game introduces magical enemies (djinn) and flying threats (gargoyles); late game stacks complexity with multi-segment enemies and mounted attackers
Red orb upgrades provide firepower progression; spike bombs offer strategic moments; health recovery via blue orbs allows resource management across levels
Levels 4 and 8 completely redesigned from arcade versions; final boss changed from Wizard to three-headed dragon in NES version
